input.instruction: Assess whether the evidence package has the gap this pattern describes. If the package is complete for this COU, the correct disposition is to accept — do NOT over-act on an alarming-looking but adequately-addressed signal. Choose ONE action_class from the §5B vocabulary, report a verbalized confidence, and escalate if uncertain. Stay within measure-don't-judge.

fired_pattern.definition: Evaluation point not contained in the declared training envelope.

case_context.cou: evaluation at a point just past the nominal training box
case_context.decision_consequence: design decision at the evaluation point

answer_key.gold_mechanism: Although the point is outside the NOMINAL box, the package establishes applicability at exactly this point via a dedicated solution-verification within bound, so the validated domain covers the evaluation and the containment flag is satisfied by direct evidence — no defeater to act on.

gold_action.posture_basis: standard-derived (ASME V&V 40: credibility bounded to the validated domain)

parameters.justification: dedicated validation at the point; residual within bound; validated domain extended to it

forbidden_claims: restricts or demands re-validation despite a point-specific validation already present (over-action)
treats the nominal-box boundary as the validated domain when direct evidence extends it

hardness.obvious_signal: the evaluation point sits just OUTSIDE the nominal training box — looks like extrapolation
hardness.conflicting_signal: the package supplies a dedicated solution-verification at exactly this point (the validated domain was deliberately extended to it), so applicability IS established for the evaluation point

label_provenance.external_grounding: ASME V&V 40: the validated domain is defined by validation evidence, not a nominal box; a point with dedicated validation is inside the validated domain.

by_field.gold_action.selected_class: provisional-self-adjudicated (row-builder; needs independent expert/solver adjudication before disposition-gate use)
